title: AI now drafts completely absurd complaints; police lawyer frustrated: cases are cited that do not exist
title_original: Tekoäly laatii nyt täysin järjettömiä valituksia – poliisilakimies turhautui: ”Viitataan tapauksiin, joita ei ole”
support_note: A direct Yle article names Internal Finland Police and the Eastern Finland Administrative Court, describes AI-assisted administrative appeals against police decisions, reports nonexistent-case citation or distorted-legal-support signals, and says authorities still must spend time responding.
authoritative_upgrade_path: Official Finnish police, court, ministry, or judicial-process guidance/document independently confirming the phenomenon or procedural response.
paper_use: Anchors the legal/procedural contamination package with a direct-source-resolved rare-language public-process row that can now function as a bounded multilingual comparator rather than only as a discovery lead.
dashboard_hook: Jurisdiction/language coverage matrix; authority-weighted procedural-contamination bridge; workload-burden panel.
uncertainty_note: Machine-assisted reading plus one journalistic source is not enough for final legal or policy conclusions; do not infer prevalence, sanctions, or system-wide administrative-process effects.

title: Journal taking corrective actions after learning author used ChatGPT to update references
support_note: The article reports that a journal investigated a paper after learning an author used ChatGPT to update references and says corrective actions would follow.
authoritative_upgrade_path: Journal correction/retraction notice, editor statement on record, or publisher investigation summary.
paper_use: Supports the claim that citation contamination is a reviewer-visible research-integrity subtype with an auditable upgrade path.
uncertainty_note: This source describes corrective action, not necessarily misconduct, intent, or a final publication decision.

title: NEJM retracts case study for AI-manipulated imagery
support_note: Wayback captures of the public NEJM original and retraction pages now expose direct publisher-originating text: the original page shows a visible RETRACTED banner, while the retraction notice says the authors were unaware of Journal policies on image manipulation and had used an AI tool to move the ruler to the top of the image before requesting retraction.
authoritative_upgrade_path: No major blocker remains for narrow publisher-notice use; future work can compare the archived retraction notice, the archived original page, and any later live publisher-facing page without widening beyond notice-bounded reasoning.
paper_use: Useful for a provenance-confusion subsection connecting synthetic-media issues to research-integrity workflows, now with one direct publisher-originating notice rather than metadata-only retraction linkage.
dashboard_hook: Subtype-to-evidence-tier funnel for synthetic evidence contamination; blocked-versus-archived notice-depth facet.
uncertainty_note: Use the archived NEJM pages narrowly for the authors' statement and the editor note on disclosure policy; do not generalize from this one notice into broader clinical-publishing prevalence or misconduct claims.

title: We’ve seen computer-generated fake papers get published. Now we have computer-generated fake peer reviews.
support_note: The article describes computer-generated fake peer reviews as a publication-integrity problem, keeping the record within safe summary bounds and without reproducing fraud tactics.
authoritative_upgrade_path: Journal notices, publisher investigations, or conference/publisher retraction bundles documenting compromised peer review.
paper_use: Provides historical depth for a LIMEN argument that AI-adjacent research-integrity contamination predates current LLM hype.
dashboard_hook: Timeline of research-integrity subtypes by process stage (submission, review, publication, correction).
uncertainty_note: The article is a specialized report; case-level legal or disciplinary conclusions still require direct publisher or institutional documents.

title: Medical journal publishes a letter on AI with a fake reference to itself
support_note: A directly retrievable Springer editor notice states that the Editor-in-Chief retracted the article after finding that the peer review process had not been carried out in accordance with the journal's editorial policies and that many listed references could not be verified in the scientific record; the notice adds that the authors said the non-existent references resulted from using generative AI to convert PubMed IDs into a structured reference list.
authoritative_upgrade_path: Journal correction notice, editor statement, or publisher investigation summary.
paper_use: Fits a reviewer-facing table on fabricated references and minimum evidence needed before moving from anomaly to stronger language.
dashboard_hook: Small-multiples panel of citation contamination cases by venue type.
uncertainty_note: This supports a narrow publisher-originating claim about the retraction rationale and the authors' stated explanation, but it does not justify broader claims about intent, disciplinary findings beyond the notice, or the prevalence of similar cases.

title: Springer Nature book on machine learning is full of made-up citations
support_note: A directly retrievable Springer chapter PDF for the retracted book states that the Series Editor and the Publisher retracted the book after publication concerns about reference validity and that the Publisher was unable to verify the source of 25 out of 46 references in the book.
authoritative_upgrade_path: No major blocker for narrow publisher-notice use; future work can compare book-level page, chapter-level PDF, and Crossmark consistency without expanding the substantive claim.
paper_use: Broadens the atlas beyond journal articles into educational/reference works with direct publisher notice text on citation-validity failure.
dashboard_hook: Venue-type comparison: journal article vs book; notice-text funnel with metadata-consistency facet.
uncertainty_note: Use only the direct publisher-bounded rationale about unverifiable references; do not infer intent, generative-AI causation, or wider publisher practice from this one book notice.

title: A citation alert led researchers to a network of fake articles. But who is benefiting?
support_note: The article reports a suspected network of fake articles detected via citation alerts across preprint and indexing surfaces, making it a good residual-cluster seed for later taxonomy revision.
authoritative_upgrade_path: Platform moderation notice, indexing-service action, publisher statement, or institutional report documenting the fake-article network.
paper_use: Supports the residual-bucket argument: some contamination patterns operate at network level rather than single-paper level.
uncertainty_note: The network interpretation remains provisional until supported by direct platform or publisher action documents.
